title: Debounced Button Counter
subtitle: Count button presses without double-counting bounce.

hints.content: Real buttons don't switch cleanly — they "bounce" and can register several presses in a few milliseconds. This button simulates that.
Track the time of the last accepted press with millis(). Ignore new presses that happen within ~50ms of the last one.
if (digitalRead(2) == LOW && millis() - lastPressMs > 50) { count++; lastPressMs = millis(); Serial.println("Count: " + String(count)); }

narrative.briefing: Mechanical buttons physically bounce when pressed, sending several rapid on/off signals instead of one clean click. Build a counter that ignores the bounce and counts real presses.
narrative.context: Debouncing is one of the first "real world is messy" lessons in embedded programming — the same pattern shows up reading sensors, encoders, and switches on every robot you'll ever build.
narrative.successMessage: Your counter ignored the bounce and counted real presses — that's a debounce filter, and you'll use that trick constantly.
